name: 1935 Chocolate Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve yeast in warm milk; let stand 5 minutes.
2. Sift together 1-1/2 cups flour, sugar, cocoa, and salt; set aside.
3. Combine dissolved yeast, egg, shortening, pecans, and vanilla; mix well.
4. Gradually add the flour mixture to make a soft dough.
5. Turn dough out onto a surface sprinkled with remaining 1/2 cup flour; knead for 10 minutes or until smooth and elastic.
6. Place in a well-greased bowl, turning to grease top. Cover and let rise in a warm place (85°F), free from drafts, 1 hour or until doubled in bulk.
7. Punch dough down; turn out onto a floured surface, and shape into a loaf.
8. Place in a greased 7-1/2 x 3 x 2-inch loaf pan. Brush top with oil; cover and repeat rising procedure 1 hour.
9. Bake at 375°F for 40 minutes or until loaf sounds hollow when tapped.
10. Remove bread from pan immediately; cool on wire rack.
